title: cochemiea saboae subsp. haudeana
intro: Cochemiea saboae subsp. haudeana, also known as Haude's cochineal, is a type of cactus plant that belongs to the genus Cochemiea. It is a succulent plant native to Mexico, known for its vibrant red flowers and edible cochineal insects that live on its stems, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The cochineal insects have medicinal properties due to their high concentration of carotenoid pigments, specifically lycopene, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The insects can be used to prepare herbal remedies such as tinctures, capsules, and infusions, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection.
